message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="yellow" summary="Agreeing on cancel-edit, empty-state, atomicity">
Carson, thanks for the thorough hypermedia review. A few responses:

**On the cancel-edit pattern**: You are right that `hx-select="main > *"` to re-fetch the full page is heavier than it needs to be. A dedicated endpoint returning just the `context_item.html` fragment for a specific context would be more efficient and more in the spirit of locality of behavior. I would support adding that as a refinement — either as a deferred item or in the next step if we are touching contexts again.

**On the empty-state JavaScript**: I agree this is one of those cases where HTMX alone cannot handle it cleanly. The child-count-based toggle is inherently imperative. I kept it minimal — just toggling a class based on whether the list has children. If an HTMX extension emerges that handles this declaratively, we should adopt it, but for now the JS approach is the pragmatic choice.

**On atomicity**: Fully aligned with your 7-step breakdown. That is almost exactly the decomposition I should have followed. For Step 4 (Next Actions), which will be more complex (clarify workflow, context association), breaking it into those kinds of slices will be even more important. I will commit to following that pattern as Driver or will flag it to whoever drives next.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Luca's response addresses my specific points and commits to the atomicity improvement. Good alignment. A brief acknowledgment is appropriate.

input.content:
Sounds good. On the cancel-edit pattern: let us add it to deferred items. When it gets picked up, the fix is straightforward — a `GET /contexts/:id` endpoint that returns just the `context_item.html` fragment when it sees the `HX-Request` header (and redirects to `/contexts` for non-HTMX requests). Same pattern we already use elsewhere. Five minutes of work, keeps the cancel interaction local.

On the empty-state JS: agreed, pragmatic choice. No action needed there.

Looking forward to seeing the smaller steps in Step 4.
input.summary: Ack, cancel-edit to deferred, ready for Step 4

routing.summary: Ack, cancel-edit to deferred, ready for Step 4

content.text: Still waiting on Lea, Heydon, and Marty to complete the retro. We have 7 of 9 team members who have weighed in so far.
